section.heading: Consuls, vice consuls, and members of a diplomatic mission as defendant
section.chapeau: The district courts shall have original jurisdiction, exclusive of the courts of the States, of all civil actions and proceedings against—

paragraph.content: consuls or vice consuls of foreign states; or
members of a mission or members of their families (as such terms are defined in section 2 of the Diplomatic Relations Act).

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: Words “civil action” were substituted for “suits,” and “all suits and proceedings” in view of Rule 2 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Section 2 of the Diplomatic Relations Act, referred to in par. (2), is classified to section 254a of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1978—Pub. L. 95–393 substituted “Consuls, vice consuls, and members of a diplomatic mission as defendant” for “Consuls and vice consuls as defendants” in section catchline, designated existing provisions as introductory provision preceding par. (1), and in such introductory provision as so designated, substituted “civil actions and proceedings against—” for “actions and proceedings against consuls or vice consuls of foreign states”, and added pars. (1) and (2).
1949—Act May 24, 1949, substituted “of all actions and proceedings” for “of any civil action”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 1978 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 95–393 effective at end of ninety-day period beginning on Sept. 30, 1978, see section 9 of Pub. L. 95–393, set out as an Effective Date note under section 254a of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse.
